name: Harbourfront
summary: The main waterfront band for public events, lakefront walking, official-style activations, and visitor-friendly gathering points south of Union.
later: Venue/event pins, waterfront crowd status, family-friendly tags, ferry-terminal notes, and nearby watch-spot recommendations.

name: Union
summary: Union Station, Scotiabank Arena, PATH exits, and the easiest downtown landing area for visitors.
later: Transit alerts, walk-time bands, late-night food, and first-stop recommendations.

name: Liberty
summary: The western downtown block closest to Exhibition Place and BMO Field, useful for matchday movement.
later: Stadium approach routes, patio clusters, pre-game flow, and post-game flow.

name: King West
summary: Dense nightlife strip that will probably matter once watch-party venues get layered on.
later: Sound-on venues, reservation status, 19+ tags, and line/capacity reports.

name: Queen West
summary: Useful for lowkey watch spots, food, cafes, patios, and spillover from downtown crowds.
later: Lowkey tags, cafe/pub split, outdoor seating, and crowd vibe.

name: Ossington
summary: A natural place to track country-specific crowds later, especially Portuguese and broader football communities.
later: Fanbase tags, Portuguese crowd signals, and smaller venue discoveries.

name: Kensington
role: Dense food and late-day wandering block
summary: A central, walkable zone that can become useful for food, casual screens, and pre/post-match wandering.
later: Food-first venues, lowkey screens, late-night options, and pedestrian notes.

name: Entertainment
summary: The most obvious central nightlife area, good for big screens, crowds, and event-style listings later.
later: Big-screen venues, cover charges, sound-on status, and crowd reports.

name: Financial
summary: The weekday worker zone: useful for early matches, after-work crowds, and PATH-adjacent viewing.
later: Office crowd timing, lunch specials, indoor routes, and quick-walk picks.

name: Dundas
summary: A high-traffic downtown node that matters for visitors, transit, and public-screen possibilities.
later: Public screening notes, family-safe timing, and transit crowd alerts.

name: St. Lawrence
summary: A useful east-core pocket with St Lawrence Market, pubs, and walkable access from Union.
later: Market-area food notes, east-core pubs, and walk-from-Union guidance.

name: Distillery
summary: A scenic eastern district where event-style screens and patio activations could be worth tracking.
later: Outdoor screens, event listings, family-friendly tags, and streetcar notes.

name: Riverside
summary: Keeps the map from becoming only the tourist core and gives us room for east-side community listings.
later: Community venues, neighborhood watch parties, and lowkey finds.

name: Yorkville
summary: The upper downtown block with international restaurants, pubs, and transit access around Bloor.
later: Restaurant screens, international crowd tags, and subway access.

name: The Village
summary: A distinct downtown nightlife zone that deserves its own block before venues get added.
later: Pride-friendly venues, nightlife timing, and patio/event notes.
